demographics.audit_notes: Values align well with 1990 Decennial Census for Tract 3.05, which is used as a proxy for Hyde Park West. New fields are sourced from the same census data or estimated based on it.
Values appear to be a reasonable linear interpolation between the 1990 and 2000 census data points for Tract 3.05.
Values align well with 2000 Decennial Census for Tract 3.05.
Year falls before reliable ACS 5-year estimates. Values are a plausible interpolation between 2000 and 2010 census data.
Values align well with 2010 Decennial Census and 2006-2010 ACS 5-Year estimates for Tract 3.05.
Values are plausible and align with trends from the ACS 2011-2015 5-Year estimates for Tract 3.05.
Values align well with 2020 Decennial Census and 2016-2020 ACS 5-Year estimates for Tract 3.05.
Values are consistent with ACS 2017-2021 5-Year estimates.
Values are consistent with the latest available ACS 2018-2022 5-Year estimates.
Values are projections based on recent ACS trends. Confidence is medium as they are not based on released data.

property.audit_notes: CORRECTED: Median Home Value adjusted from $155k to $145k to better align with Census 1990 ($140k). CORRECTED: Median Rent adjusted from $650 to $500; original value was implausibly high compared to Census 1990 data (~$450).

socioeconomic.audit_notes: CORRECTED: Median Household Income adjusted from $41,000 to $30,000. The original value was over 35% higher than the 1990 Census value for Tract 3.05 (~$29k).
Values are plausible interpolations based on corrected 1990 and 2000 census data. Unemployment rate reflects the mid-90s economic recovery.
CORRECTED: Median Household Income adjusted from $65,000 to $58,000 to align with 2000 Census data for Tract 3.05. Unemployment rate reflects the dot-com boom.
Values are plausible interpolations. MHI adjusted downward from $72k to reflect corrected 2000/2010 values.
CORRECTED: Median Household Income adjusted from $81,000 to $75,000 to align with ACS 2006-2010 data. Unemployment rate reflects the Great Recession.
Values align well with ACS 2011-2015 data. MHI of $98k is plausible and within range of ACS estimate (~$92k).
Values align well with ACS 2016-2020 data. MHI of $125k is plausible and within range of ACS estimate (~$120k). Unemployment reflects COVID-19 impact.
Values are consistent with ACS 2017-2021 5-Year estimates.
Values are consistent with the latest available ACS 2018-2022 5-Year estimates.
